title: Preserving MWPM-Decodability in Fault-Equivalent Rewrites
summary_bullets: This paper formalises how ZX-calculus rewrites change the decoding problem by introducing detector-aware rewrites that track local detecting Pauli webs and their boundary actions. It defines matchability-preserving rewrites which guarantee that minimum-weight perfect matching (MWPM) decodability is retained, and proves any phase-free ZX diagram can be fault-equivalently extracted into a quantum circuit while preserving matchability. Using these constructions the authors build efficiently decodable, fault-tolerant syndrome-extraction circuits for matchable codes (e.g. surface-code-like spacetime codes).

Yanbei would care because his interests in quantum measurement, quantum noise and quantum error correction intersect where circuit-level noise propagation and real-time decoders meet experiment: preserving MWPM-decodability during circuit synthesis directly impacts the design of implementable syndrome-extraction and readout circuits that require low-latency, reliable classical decoding. The detector-aware, fault-equivalent rewrite framework gives a principled way to generate circuits whose noise-behaviour and decoder complexity are predictable, which is valuable for tabletop and optomechanical experiments (and any application where QND/back-action considerations and fast decoding are critical) and complements his interest in scalable QEC and experimental signatures that depend on preserving quantum coherence under realistic operations.

Keywords/topics: ZX calculus, detector-aware rewrites, matchability, MWPM decoding, fault-equivalent rewrites, Pauli webs, syndrome extraction, surface code, stabiliser codes, efficient classical decoding. Published on arXiv 19 Mar 2026 (very recent).
